text.Rotherham: They have cut down her forest Declareth Yahweh, Surely he cannot be searched out,—For they have outnumbered locusts, and cannot be counted.

text.NRSV: Daughter Egypt shall be put to shame; she shall be handed over to a people from the north.
text.Rotherham: Put to shame is the daughter of Egypt,—She hath been delivered into the hand of the people of the North.

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, said: See, I am bringing punishment upon Amon of Thebes, and Pharaoh, and Egypt and her gods and her kings, upon Pharaoh and those who trust in him.
text.Rotherham: Saith Yahweh of host God of Israel,—Behold me! bringing punishment—against Amon of No, and upon Pharaoh and upon Egypt, and upon her gods and upon her kings,—Even upon Pharaoh, and upon all that trust in him;

text.NRSV: I will hand them over to those who seek their lives, to King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon and his officers. Afterward Egypt shall be inhabited as in the days of old, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will deliver them—Into the hand of them who are seeking their life, Even into the hand of Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon And into the hand of his servants,—And after that, shall it be inhabited as in the days of old Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: But as for you, have no fear, my servant Jacob, and do not be dismayed, O Israel; for I am going to save you from far away, and your offspring from the land of their captivity. Jacob shall return and have quiet and ease, and no one shall make him afraid.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, do not fear, O my Servant Jacob Nor be dismayed O Israel, For behold me! Saving thee from afar, And thy seed from the land of their captivity,—So shall Jacob return and be quiet and shall rest, With none to occasion alarm.

text.NRSV: As for you, have no fear, my servant Jacob, says the Lord, for I am with you. I will make an end of all the nations among which I have banished you, but I will not make an end of you. I will chastise you in just measure, and I will by no means leave you unpunished.
text.Rotherham: Thou, do not fear, O my Servant Jacob, Urgeth Yahweh, For, with thee, am I: Though I make an end of all the nations whither I have driven thee, Yet of thee, will I not make an end, But will correct thee in measure, And not hold thee, guiltless,

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to the prophet Jeremiah concerning the Philistines, before Pharaoh struck down Gaza.
text.Rotherham: So much of the word of Yahweh as came unto Jeremiah the prophet, Against the Philistines,—before Pharaoh smote Gaza:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: See, waters are rising out of the north and shall become an overflowing torrent; they shall overflow the land and all that fills it, the city and those who live in it. People shall cry out, and all the inhabitants of the land shall wail.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—Lo! waters rising from the North And they shall become a torrent overflowing, Which shall overflow The land and the fulness thereof, The city, and the dwellers therein,—Then shall men make outcry, And all the inhabitants of the land, howl.

text.NRSV: At the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his stallions, at the clatter of his chariots and the rumbling of their wheels, parents do not turn back for children, so feeble are their hands,
text.Rotherham: Because of the sound of the tramping of the hoofs of his chargers, Because of the rushing of his chariots, the rumbling of his wheels Fathers shall not turn, to children, Because of the unnerving of their hands;

text.NRSV: because of the day that is coming to destroy all the Philistines, to cut off from Tyre and Sidon every helper that remains. For the Lord is destroying the Philistines, the remnant of the coastland of Caphtor.
text.Rotherham: On account of the day that cometh to lay waste all the Philistines, To cut off from Tyre and Zidon every escaped one that could have helped,—For Yahweh is laying waste the Philistines, The remnant of the Coastland of Caphtor.

text.NRSV: Baldness has come upon Gaza, Ashkelon is silenced. O remnant of their power! How long will you gash yourselves?
text.Rotherham: Baldness hath come, upon Gaza, Silenced is Ashkelon the remnant of their vale,—How long, wilt thou cut thyself?

text.NRSV: Ah, sword of the Lord! How long until you are quiet? Put yourself into your scabbard, rest and be still!
text.Rotherham: Ah! thou sword of Yahweh, How long, wilt thou not be quiet? Withdraw into thy scabbard, Rest thyself and be still.

text.NRSV: How can it be quiet when the Lord has given it a charge? Against Ashkelon and against the seashore—there he has appointed it.
text.Rotherham: How, can it be quiet, when, Yahweh, hath given it a charge,—against Ashkelon and against the shore of the sea? There, hath he appointed it!

text.NRSV: Concerning Moab. Thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: Alas for Nebo, for it is laid waste! Kiriathaim is put to shame, it is taken; the fortress is put to shame and broken down;
text.Rotherham: Of Moab,—Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts God of Israel,—Alas for Nebo, for it is laid waste, put to shame—captured, is Kiriathaim, put to shame is Misgab, and dismayed.

text.NRSV: the renown of Moab is no more. In Heshbon they planned evil against her: “Come, let us cut her off from being a nation!” You also, O Madmen, shall be brought to silence; the sword shall pursue you.
text.Rotherham: No more, is the praise of Moab, In Heshbon, have they devised against her, calamity, Come, and let us cut her off from being a nation,—Even thou, Madmen also, shalt be silenced, After thee, shall march the sword.

text.NRSV: Hark! a cry from Horonaim, “Desolation and great destruction!”
text.Rotherham: A voice of outcry, from Horonaim,—Wasting and great destruction!

text.NRSV: Moab is broken; her little ones cry out.
text.Rotherham: Moab is broken: Her little ones, have caused to be heard an outcry.

text.NRSV: For at the ascent of Luhith they go up weeping bitterly; for at the descent of Horonaim they have heard the distressing cry of destruction.
text.Rotherham: For the ascent of Luhith, with weeping, one ascendeth—with weeping,—For in the descent of Horonaim, the distress of the outcry of destruction, have they heard:

text.NRSV: Flee! Save yourselves! Be like a wild ass in the desert!
text.Rotherham: Flee deliver your own lives,—Then shall the women be as a shrub in the desert.

text.NRSV: Surely, because you trusted in your strongholds and your treasures, you also shall be taken; Chemosh shall go out into exile, with his priests and his attendants.
text.Rotherham: For seeing thou hast trusted in thy works and in thy treasures, Thou too, shalt be captured,—Then shall Chemosh go forth into captivity, His priests and his princes together.

text.NRSV: The destroyer shall come upon every town, and no town shall escape; the valley shall perish, and the plain shall be destroyed, as the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: And the spoiler shall enter into every city, And not, a city, shall escape, Then shall perish the vale Then be destroyed the table-land,—As Yahweh hath said.

text.NRSV: Set up a tombstone for Moab, for she will surely be laid in ruins; her towns shall become a desolation, with no inhabitant in them.
text.Rotherham: Give wing to Moab, For she must, fly away; And her cities To desolation, shall be turned, With no inhabitant therein.

text.NRSV: Accursed is the one who is slack in doing the Lord’s work, and accursed is the one who keeps back the sword from bloodshed.
text.Rotherham: Accursed be he that doeth the business of Yahweh carelessly; And, accursed be he that withholdeth his sword, from blood!

text.NRSV: Moab has been at ease from his youth, settled like wine on its dregs; he has not been emptied from vessel to vessel, nor has he gone into exile; therefore his flavor has remained and his aroma is unchanged.
text.Rotherham: Moab, hath been at ease from his youth, And settled is he upon his lees, And hath not been poured out from vessel to vessel, Nor into captivity, hath gone,—For this cause, hath his flavour stood still within him, And his scent is unchanged.

text.NRSV: Therefore, the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I shall send to him decanters to tip him over, and empty his vessels, and break his jars in pieces.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh, When I will send to him tilters and they shall tilt him,—And his vessels, shall they empty, And their jars, shall they dash in pieces.

text.NRSV: Then Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh, as the house of Israel was ashamed of Bethel, their confidence.
text.Rotherham: Then shall Moab, be ashamed, of, Chemosh,—Just as, the sons of Israel, were ashamed of Bethel their confidence.

text.NRSV: How can you say, “We are heroes and mighty warriors”?
text.Rotherham: How can ye say, Heroes, are we? and Men of might for the war?

text.NRSV: The destroyer of Moab and his towns has come up, and the flower of his young men has gone down to slaughter, says the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Spoiled is Moab And her cities, hath he ascended, And the choice of his young men, have gone down to the slaughter,—Declareth The King, Yahweh of hosts, is his Name.

text.NRSV: The calamity of Moab is near at hand, and his doom approaches swiftly.
text.Rotherham: Near is the doom of Moab, to come,—And, his calamity, hasteth greatly.

text.NRSV: Mourn over him, all you his neighbors, and all who know his name; say, “How the mighty scepter is broken, the glorious staff!”
text.Rotherham: Lament ye for him—All that are round about him, and All that know his name,—Say, How is broken—The staff of strength! The rod of beauty!

text.NRSV: Come down from glory, and sit on the parched ground, enthroned daughter Dibon! For the destroyer of Moab has come up against you; he has destroyed your strongholds.
text.Rotherham: Come down from glory, and sit in thirst, O inhabitress daughter of Dibon; For the spoiler of Moab, hath come up against thee, He hath laid in ruins thy strongholds.

text.NRSV: Stand by the road and watch, you inhabitant of Aroer! Ask the man fleeing and the woman escaping; say, “What has happened?”
text.Rotherham: Near the way, take thy stand and keep outlook, O inhabitress of Aroer: Ask—him that fleeth, and Her that hath escaped, Say, What hath happened?

text.NRSV: Moab is put to shame, for it is broken; wail and cry! Tell it by the Arnon, that Moab is laid waste.
text.Rotherham: Confounded is Moab! For it is broken down! Howl and make outcry,—Tell ye in Arnon, That Moab is spoiled;

text.NRSV: Judgment has come upon the tableland, upon Holon, and Jahzah, and Mephaath,
text.Rotherham: And, judgment, hath come Upon the country of the table-land,—Upon Holon, and Upon Jazer and Upon Mephaath;

text.NRSV: and Dibon, and Nebo, and Beth-diblathaim,
text.Rotherham: and Upon Dibon, and Upon Nebo, and Upon Beth-diblathaim;

text.NRSV: and Kiriathaim, and Beth-gamul, and Beth-meon,
text.Rotherham: and Upon Kiriathaim, and Upon Beth-gamul, and Upon Beth-meon;

text.NRSV: and Kerioth, and Bozrah, and all the towns of the land of Moab, far and near.
text.Rotherham: and Upon Kerioth, and Upon Bozrah,—Yea, upon all the cities of the land of Moab, far and near.

text.NRSV: The horn of Moab is cut off, and his arm is broken, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Cut off is the horn of Moab, Yea, his arm, is broken,—Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Make him drunk, because he magnified himself against the Lord; let Moab wallow in his vomit; he too shall be held in derision.
text.Rotherham: Make ye him drunken, Because against Yahweh, hath he magnified himself; So shall, Moab, stagger, into his own vomit, And shall become a derision, even he!

text.NRSV: Was not Israel a derision to you? Was he found among thieves, that whenever you spoke of him you shook your head?
text.Rotherham: And was it not, a derision, that Israel became to thee? And was it not among thieves, he was found? For, as often as thou didst speak of him, thou didst wag the head!

text.NRSV: Leave the towns, and live on the rock, O inhabitants of Moab! Be like the dove that nests on the sides of the mouth of a gorge.
text.Rotherham: Leave ye the cities and dwell in the cliff, Ye inhabitants of Moab; And become ye as a dove, that maketh her nest in the further-side of the fissure’s mouth.

text.NRSV: We have heard of the pride of Moab— he is very proud!— of his loftiness, his pride, and his arrogance, and the haughtiness of his heart.
text.Rotherham: We have heard the arrogance of Moab—proud exceedingly: His loftiness and his arrogance and his majesty, and his elevation of heart!

text.NRSV: I myself know his insolence, says the Lord; his boasts are false, his deeds are false.
text.Rotherham: I know—Declareth Yahweh His passion—that ’tis Unjust! His boastings, that Untrue have they made [them] !

text.NRSV: Therefore I wail for Moab; I cry out for all Moab; for the men of Kir-heres I mourn.
text.Rotherham: For this cause over Moab, will I howl, And for all Moab, will I make outcry,—For the men of Kir-heres, must one moan!

text.NRSV: More than for Jazer I weep for you, O vine of Sibmah! Your branches passed over the sea, they reached as far as Jazer; upon your summer fruits and your vintage the destroyer has fallen.
text.Rotherham: Beyond the weeping of Jazer, will I weep for thee O vine of Sibmah, Thy tendrils, have gone over the sea, Unto the sea of Jazer, have they reached, Upon thy summer fruits And upon thy vintage, the spoiler, hath fallen.

text.NRSV: Gladness and joy have been taken away from the fruitful land of Moab; I have stopped the wine from the wine presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy; the shouting is no shout of joy.
text.Rotherham: So shall be withdrawn gladness and exultation—From the fruitful field and From the land of Moab,—And wine from the vats, have I caused to fail, They shall not tread with shouting, The shouting shall be no shouting!

text.NRSV: Heshbon and Elealeh cry out; as far as Jahaz they utter their voice, from Zoar to Horonaim and Eglath-shelishiyah. For even the waters of Nimrim have become desolate.
text.Rotherham: From the outcry of Heshbon, Even unto Elealeh Even unto Jahaz, have they given forth their voice, From Zoar even unto Horonaim, the third Eglath,—For, even the waters of Nimrim, to utter desolation, have been turned:

text.NRSV: And I will bring to an end in Moab, says the Lord, those who offer sacrifice at a high place and make offerings to their gods.
text.Rotherham: Then will I cause to cease, from Moab, Declareth Yahweh,—him that offereth at a high-place, and him that burneth incense to his gods.

text.NRSV: Therefore my heart moans for Moab like a flute, and my heart moans like a flute for the men of Kir-heres; for the riches they gained have perished.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, my heart, for Moab, like flutes, shall make plaintive sounds, Yea my heart for the men of Kir-heres! like flutes, shall make plaintive sounds, Because, the abundance he hath gotten is lost!

text.NRSV: For every head is shaved and every beard cut off; on all the hands are gashes, and on the loins is sackcloth.
text.Rotherham: For, every head, is baldness, And every beard, clipped: Upon all hands, are gashes, And upon the loins, sackcloth

text.NRSV: On all the housetops of Moab and in the squares there is nothing but lamentation; for I have broken Moab like a vessel that no one wants, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Upon all the housetops of Moab And in her broadways, it is all lamentation,—For I have broken Moab—Like a vessel wherein is no pleasure Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: How it is broken! How they wail! How Moab has turned his back in shame! So Moab has become a derision and a horror to all his neighbors.
text.Rotherham: How it is broken down! They have howled, How hath Moab turned the back, for shame,—So shall Moab become a derision and a terror to all round about him.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord: Look, he shall swoop down like an eagle, and spread his wings against Moab;
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith Yahweh, Lo! as an eagle, shall one dart along,—And shall spread out his wings towards Moab.

text.NRSV: the towns shall be taken and the strongholds seized. The heart of the warriors of Moab on that day shall be like the heart of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Captured is Kerioth, And the fortresses, are seized,—Then shall the heart of the heroes of Moab become in that day, As the heart of a woman in her pains.

text.NRSV: Moab shall be destroyed as a people, because he magnified himself against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall, Moab, be destroyed from being a people,—Because against Yahweh, hath he magnified himself.

text.NRSV: Terror, pit, and snare are before you, O inhabitant of Moab! says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Terror and pit and snare are upon thee O inhabitant of Moab Declareth Yahweh:

text.NRSV: Everyone who flees from the terror shall fall into the pit, and everyone who climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For I will bring these things upon Moab in the year of their punishment, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: He that fleeth from the face of the terror, Shall fall into the pit, And, he that getteth up out of the pit, Shall be captured by the snare,—For I will bring against her—against Moab, The year of their visitation, Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: In the shadow of Heshbon fugitives stop exhausted; for a fire has gone out from Heshbon, a flame from the house of Sihon; it has destroyed the foreheads of Moab, the scalps of the tumultuous people.
text.Rotherham: In the shadow of Heshbon, stand strengthless, the fugitives,—For, a fire, hath gone forth out of Heshbon And shall flame out of the midst of Sihon, And shall devour the beard of Moab, And the crown of the head of the proudly tumultuous.

text.NRSV: Alas for you, O Moab! The people of Chemosh have perished, for your sons have been taken captive, and your daughters into exile.
text.Rotherham: Woe to thee Moab! Lost are the people of Chemosh,—For thy sons have been taken, into captivity, And thy daughters into captivity.

text.NRSV: Yet I will restore the fortunes of Moab in the latter days, says the Lord. Thus far is the judgment on Moab.
text.Rotherham: Yet will I bring back the captivity of Moab, In the afterpart of the days, Declareth Yahweh, Thus far, is the sentence of Moab.

text.NRSV: Concerning the Ammonites. Thus says the Lord: Has Israel no sons? Has he no heir? Why then has Milcom dispossessed Gad, and his people settled in its towns?
text.Rotherham: Of the sons of Ammon-Thus saith Yahweh: Hath Israel no, sons? Hath he no, heir? Why hath, Malkam, taken possession of Gad, Or have, his people, in the cities thereof, made their dwelling?

text.NRSV: Therefore, the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I will cause the battle cry to be heard against Rabbah of the Ammonites; it shall become a desolate mound, and its villages shall be burned with fire; then Israel shall dispossess those who dispossessed him, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh When I will cause to be heard against Rabbah of the sons of Ammon, an alarm of war, So shall she become a mound of desolation, And, her villages, with fire, shall blaze,—Then shall Israel, inherit them who inherited him, Saith Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Wail, O Heshbon, for Ai is laid waste! Cry out, O daughters of Rabbah! Put on sackcloth, lament, and slash yourselves with whips! For Milcom shall go into exile, with his priests and his attendants.
text.Rotherham: Howl, O Heshbon, for Ai is spoiled Make outcry, ye daughters of Rabbah, Gird you with sackcloth, Lament ye and run to and fro among the fences,—For, Malkam, into captivity, shall go, His priests and his princes, together.

text.NRSV: Why do you boast in your strength? Your strength is ebbing, O faithless daughter. You trusted in your treasures, saying, “Who will dare to attack me?”
text.Rotherham: Why shouldst thou glory in the vales, The flowing of thy vale O apostate daughter? She who is trusting in her treasures, [saying,] Who shall invade, me?

text.NRSV: I am going to bring terror upon you, says the Lord God of hosts, from all your neighbors, and you will be scattered, each headlong, with no one to gather the fugitives.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! bringing upon thee, terror Declareth My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, From all who are round about thee,—And ye shall be driven out every man before it, And there shall be none to bring home the wanderer;

text.NRSV: But afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But afterwards, will I bring back the captivity of the sons of Ammon, Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Concerning Edom. Thus says the Lord of hosts: Is there no longer wisdom in Teman? Has counsel perished from the prudent? Has their wisdom vanished?
text.Rotherham: Of Edom—Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, Is there no longer wisdom in Teman? Hath counsel perished from the discerning? Is their wisdom corrupt?

text.NRSV: Flee, turn back, get down low, inhabitants of Dedan! For I will bring the calamity of Esau upon him, the time when I punish him.
text.Rotherham: Flee ye turn go down deep to dwell, O inhabitant of Dedan,—For the doom of Esau, have I brought in upon him The time of his visitation.

text.NRSV: If grape-gatherers came to you, would they not leave gleanings? If thieves came by night, even they would pillage only what they wanted.
text.Rotherham: If, grape-gatherers, came to thee, Would they not leave, gleanings? If, thieves in the night, Would they not have taken away what sufficed them?

text.NRSV: But as for me, I have stripped Esau bare, I have uncovered his hiding places, and he is not able to conceal himself. His offspring are destroyed, as are his kinsfolk and his neighbors; and he is no more.
text.Rotherham: But, I, have stripped Esau bare, I have uncovered his secret places, And to conceal himself, shall he not be able! Spoiled, are his seed, and his brethren, and his neighbours, and he is not.

text.NRSV: Leave your orphans, I will keep them alive; and let your widows trust in me.
text.Rotherham: Leave thy fatherless children I, will preserve them alive,—And thy widows, In me, let them trust.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord: If those who do not deserve to drink the cup must drink it, will you be the one to go unpunished? You shall not go unpunished; you must drink it.
text.Rotherham: For, thus saith Yahweh—Lo! they who had not been adjudged to drink the cup, shall surely drink, And art thou, the one to go unpunished? Thou shalt not go unpunished, but shalt surely drink.

text.NRSV: For I have sworn by myself, says the Lord, that Bozrah shall become an object of horror and ridicule, a waste, and an object of cursing; and all her towns shall be perpetual wastes.
text.Rotherham: For by myself, have I sworn, Declareth Yahweh, That, Bozrah shall become, an astonishment: a reproach and a desolation, and a contempt,—And all her cities shall become age-abiding desolations.

text.NRSV: I have heard tidings from the Lord, and a messenger has been sent among the nations: “Gather yourselves together and come against her, and rise up for battle!”
text.Rotherham: A report, have I heard from Yahweh, Yea, an envoy, throughout the nations, hath been sent,—Gather yourselves together and come against her, And arise to the battle.

text.NRSV: For I will make you least among the nations, despised by humankind.
text.Rotherham: For lo! Small, have I made thee among the nations,—Despised among men!

text.NRSV: The terror you inspire and the pride of your heart have deceived you, you who live in the clefts of the rock, who hold the height of the hill. Although you make your nest as high as the eagle’s, from there I will bring you down, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thy monstrous thing, hath deceived thee, The insolence of thy heart, O thou that inhabitest the hidden recesses of the cliff, That holdest fast the height of the hill,—Though thou set high as an eagle, thy nest, From thence, will I bring thee down, Declareth Yahweh;

text.NRSV: Edom shall become an object of horror; everyone who passes by it will be horrified and will hiss because of all its disasters.
text.Rotherham: So shall, Edom, become an astonishment,—Every one passing by her, will be astonished and hiss, over all her plagues:

text.NRSV: As when Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighbors were overthrown, says the Lord, no one shall live there, nor shall anyone settle in it.
text.Rotherham: Like the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and her neighbours, Saith Yahweh,—There shall not dwell there—a man, Nor sojourn therein—any son of the earth-born.

text.NRSV: Like a lion coming up from the thickets of the Jordan against a perennial pasture, I will suddenly chase Edom away from it; and I will appoint over it whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who can summon me? Who is the shepherd who can stand before me?
text.Rotherham: Lo! like a lion, will he come up from the majesty of the Jordan, unto the pasture perennial, But I will wink—I will make him run away therefrom, Who then shall be the Chosen One whom over it, I may set in charge? For who shall be like unto, me? And who shall appoint me, a time? And who is the Shepherd that shall stand before, me?

text.NRSV: Therefore hear the plan that the Lord has made against Edom and the purposes he has formed against the inhabitants of Teman: Surely the little ones of the flock shall be dragged away; surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, hear ye The counsel of Yahweh which he hath counseled against Edom, And his devices which he hath devised against the inhabitants of Teman,—Surely the little ones of the flock shall drag them away, Surely he will cause their fold to be astounded over them.

text.NRSV: At the sound of their fall the earth shall quake; the sound of their cry shall be heard at the Red Sea.
text.Rotherham: At the noise of their fall, hath trembled the earth, At the outcry, in the Red Sea, was heard its noise.

text.NRSV: Look, one shall mount up and swoop down like an eagle, and spread his wings against Bozrah; and the heart of the warriors of Edom on that day shall be like the heart of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Lo! as an eagle, he shall mount and dart, and spread his wings over Bozrah,—So shall the heart of the heroes of Edom, in that day, become as the heart of a woman in her pain.

text.NRSV: Concerning Damascus. Hamath and Arpad are confounded, for they have heard bad news; they melt in fear, they are troubled like the sea that cannot be quiet.
text.Rotherham: Of Damascus. Turned pale have Hamath and Arpad, For, a calamitous report, have they heard—they tremble,—In the sea, is anxiety, it cannot, rest.

text.NRSV: Damascus has become feeble, she turned to flee, and panic seized her; anguish and sorrows have taken hold of her, as of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: Enfeebled is Damascus She hath turned to flee But terror, hath seized her,—Anguish and pangs, have seized her as a woman in childbirth.

text.NRSV: How the famous city is forsaken, the joyful town!
text.Rotherham: Alas! is she not forsaken—The city so praised! The citadel I rejoiced in!

text.NRSV: Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares, and all her soldiers shall be destroyed on that day, says the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall her young men fall in her broadways,—And, all her men of war, shall be silent in that day, Declareth Yahweh of hosts;

text.NRSV: And I will kindle a fire at the wall of Damascus, and it shall devour the strongholds of Ben-hadad.
text.Rotherham: Then will I kindle a fire in the walls of Damascus,—And it shall devour the palaces of Ben-hadad.

text.NRSV: Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor that King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon defeated. Thus says the Lord: Rise up, advance against Kedar! Destroy the people of the east!
text.Rotherham: Of Kedar. And of the kingdoms of Hazor Which Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon smote—Thus, saith Yahweh,—Arise ye go up against Kedar, So shall they spoil the sons of the East:

text.NRSV: Take their tents and their flocks, their curtains and all their goods; carry off their camels for yourselves, and a cry shall go up: “Terror is all around!”
text.Rotherham: Their tents and their flocks, shall they take, And their curtains and all their baggage and their camels, shall they carry off for themselves,—And shall cry out unto them, Terror round about!

text.NRSV: Flee, wander far away, hide in deep places, O inhabitants of Hazor! says the Lord. For King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon has made a plan against you and formed a purpose against you.
text.Rotherham: Flee remove far away, go deep to dwell, Ye inhabitants of Hazor, Urgeth Yahweh; For Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon—hath counseled against you, a counsel, And devised against you a device.

text.NRSV: Rise up, advance against a nation at ease, that lives securely, says the Lord, that has no gates or bars, that lives alone.
text.Rotherham: Arise ye, go up against a nation at ease—dwelling securely, Commandeth Yahweh,—Having neither doors nor bars, Alone, do they dwell:

text.NRSV: Their camels shall become booty, their herds of cattle a spoil. I will scatter to every wind those who have shaven temples, and I will bring calamity upon them from every side, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall their camels, become, a prey, And the throng of their cattle, a spoil, Then will I scatter them to every wind even the clipt-beards,—And from all sides of him, will I bring in their doom Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Hazor shall become a lair of jackals, an everlasting waste; no one shall live there, nor shall anyone settle in it.
text.Rotherham: So shall Hazor become A habitation of jackals An astonishment unto times age-abiding: There shall not dwell there—a man, Nor sojourn therein—a son of the earth-born.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to the prophet Jeremiah concerning Elam, at the beginning of the reign of King Zedekiah of Judah.
text.Rotherham: So much of the word of Yahweh as came unto Jeremiah the prophet, Against Elam,—in the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah saying:—

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I am going to break the bow of Elam, the mainstay of their might;
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, Behold me! breaking the bow of Elam,—The beginning of their might;

text.NRSV: and I will bring upon Elam the four winds from the four quarters of heaven; and I will scatter them to all these winds, and there shall be no nation to which the exiles from Elam shall not come.
text.Rotherham: Then will I bring in against Elam four winds from the four quarters of the heavens, And will scatter them to all these winds,—And there shall be no nation whither the outcasts of Elam, shall not come;

text.NRSV: I will terrify Elam before their enemies, and before those who seek their life; I will bring disaster upon them, my fierce anger, says the Lord. I will send the sword after them, until I have consumed them;
text.Rotherham: And I will cause Elam to be dismayed Before their enemies Even before them who are seeking their life, So will I bring upon them calamity, Even the glow of mine anger Declareth Yahweh,—And will send after them the sword, until I have made an end of them;

text.NRSV: and I will set my throne in Elam, and destroy their king and officials, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And I will set my throne in Elam,—And will destroy, from thence king and princes, Declareth Yahweh;

text.NRSV: But in the latter days I will restore the fortunes of Elam, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But it shall come to pass In the afterpart of the days, That I will bring back the captivity of Elam, Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The word that the Lord spoke concerning Babylon, concerning the land of the Chaldeans, by the prophet Jeremiah:
text.Rotherham: The word which Yahweh spake Against Babylon Against the land of the Chaldeans, through Jeremiah the prophet:

text.NRSV: Declare among the nations and proclaim, set up a banner and proclaim; do not conceal it, say: Babylon is taken, Bel is put to shame, Merodach is dismayed. Her images are put to shame, her idols are dismayed.
text.Rotherham: Tell ye among the nations And let it be heard And lift ye up a standard, Let it be heard do not conceal: Say ye—Captured is Babylon, Confounded is Bel, Broken in pieces is Merodach, Confounded are her images, Broken down her manufactured gods;

text.NRSV: For out of the north a nation has come up against her; it shall make her land a desolation, and no one shall live in it; both human beings and animals shall flee away.
text.Rotherham: For there hath come up against her—a nation out of the North The same, shall make her land an astonishment, And there shall be none to dwell therein,—Both man and beast, have removed—have gone.

text.NRSV: In those days and at that time, says the Lord, the people of Israel shall come, they and the people of Judah together; they shall come weeping as they seek the Lord their God.
text.Rotherham: In those days, and at that time, Declareth Yahweh, Shall the sons of Israel come in, They and the sons of Judah together: Weeping as they travel, so shall they journey on, And Yahweh their God, shall they seek;

text.NRSV: They shall ask the way to Zion, with faces turned toward it, and they shall come and join themselves to the Lord by an everlasting covenant that will never be forgotten.
text.Rotherham: To Zion, shall they ask the way, Hitherward, their faces, Come and let us join ourselves unto Yahweh, In a covenant age-abiding, which shall not be forgotten.

text.NRSV: My people have been lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray, turning them away on the mountains; from mountain to hill they have gone, they have forgotten their fold.
text.Rotherham: Wandering sheep, have my people, been, Their own shepherds led them astray, On the mountains, they seduced them, From mountain to hill, have they gone, They have forgotten their couching-place.

text.NRSV: All who found them have devoured them, and their enemies have said, “We are not guilty, because they have sinned against the Lord, the true pasture, the Lord, the hope of their ancestors.”
text.Rotherham: All who found them, devoured them, And, their adversaries, said—We shall not be, guilty,—Because they have sinned, Against, Yahweh the pasturage of righteousness, Yea the hope of their fathers—Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Flee from Babylon, and go out of the land of the Chaldeans, and be like male goats leading the flock.
text.Rotherham: Remove ye out of the midst of Babylon, And out of the land of the Chaldeans, come ye forth,—And become ye like he-goats before the flock;

text.NRSV: For I am going to stir up and bring against Babylon a company of great nations from the land of the north; and they shall array themselves against her; from there she shall be taken. Their arrows are like the arrows of a skilled warrior who does not return empty-handed.
text.Rotherham: For lo! I am rousing and bringing up against Babylon a gathered host of great nations, out of the land of the North, And they shall array themselves against her, From thence, shall she be captured,—His arrows, are as of a hero making childless, None shall return empty.

text.NRSV: Chaldea shall be plundered; all who plunder her shall be sated, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall the Chaldeans become a spoil,—All her spoilers, shall be satisfied, Declareth Yahweh;

text.NRSV: Though you rejoice, though you exult, O plunderers of my heritage, though you frisk like a heifer on the grass, and neigh like stallions,
text.Rotherham: Because they used to be glad Because they used to be uproarious When plundering mine inheritance,—Because they used to caper about as a heifer at grass, And bellow like bulls.

text.NRSV: your mother shall be utterly shamed, and she who bore you shall be disgraced. Lo, she shall be the last of the nations, a wilderness, dry land, and a desert.
text.Rotherham: Your mother, hath turned very pale, She that bare you, hath turned red,—Lo! the last of nations, is—A desert, A parched land, and A waste plain,

text.NRSV: Because of the wrath of the Lord she shall not be inhabited, but shall be an utter desolation; everyone who passes by Babylon shall be appalled and hiss because of all her wounds.
text.Rotherham: Because of the vexation of Yahweh, she shall not be inhabited, But Shall become a complete desolation,—Every one passing by Babylon, shall be astonished and hiss, over all her plagues.

text.NRSV: Array yourselves against Babylon all around, all you who bend the bow; shoot at her, spare no arrows, for she has sinned against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Set yourselves in array against Babylon round about All ye who tread the bow, Shoot at her, do not spare so much as an arrow,—For against Yahweh, hath she sinned.

text.NRSV: Raise a shout against her from all sides, she has surrendered; her bulwarks have fallen, her walls are thrown down. For this is the vengeance of the Lord: take vengeance on her, do to her as she has done.
text.Rotherham: Raise a shout against her round about She hath stretched forth her hand, Fallen are her buttresses, Torn down are her walls,—Because, the avenging of Yahweh it is, Take ye vengeance upon her, As she hath done, do ye, unto her.

text.NRSV: Cut off from Babylon the sower, and the one who wields the sickle in time of harvest; because of the destroying sword, all of them shall face their own people, and all of them shall flee to their own land.
text.Rotherham: Cut ye off the sower from Babylon, And him that graspeth the sickle in the time of harvest, From the face of the sword of the oppressor, Each, to his own people will they turn, and Each, to his own land will they flee.

text.NRSV: Israel is a hunted sheep driven away by lions. First the king of Assyria devoured it, and now at the last King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon has gnawed its bones.
text.Rotherham: A sheep all alone, is Israel Lions, have driven him away,—At the first, the king of Assyria devoured him, And here, at the last, Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon hath broken his bones!

text.NRSV: Therefore, thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: I am going to punish the king of Babylon and his land, as I punished the king of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, God of Israel, Behold me! bringing punishment against the king of Babylon, and against his land,—Just as I brought punishment against the king of Assyria.

text.NRSV: I will restore Israel to its pasture, and it shall feed on Carmel and in Bashan, and its desire shall be satisfied on the hills of Ephraim and in Gilead.
text.Rotherham: So will I bring back Israel unto his own pasture, And he shall feed upon Carmel and Bashan; And in the hill country of Ephraim and Gilead, shall, his soul, be satisfied.

text.NRSV: In those days and at that time, says the Lord, the iniquity of Israel shall be sought, and there shall be none; and the sins of Judah, and none shall be found; for I will pardon the remnant that I have spared.
text.Rotherham: In those days and in that time, Declareth Yahweh, The iniquity of Israel, shall be sought, and there shall be none, And the sins of Judah, and they shall not be found; For I will grant pardon to them whom I suffer to remain.

text.NRSV: Go up to the land of Merathaim; go up against it, and against the inhabitants of Pekod and utterly destroy the last of them, says the Lord; do all that I have commanded you.
text.Rotherham: Against the land of Merathaim, go thou up against her, And against the inhabitants of Pekod,—Lay waste and devote to destruction after them Declareth Yahweh, And do according to all which I have commanded thee.

text.NRSV: The noise of battle is in the land, and great destruction!
text.Rotherham: The sound of battle is in the earth,—And a great destruction.

text.NRSV: How the hammer of the whole earth is cut down and broken! How Babylon has become a horror among the nations!
text.Rotherham: How is cut and broken the hammer of all the earth! How hath Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!

text.NRSV: I set a snare for you and you were caught, O Babylon, but you did not know it; you were found and seized, because you challenged the Lord.
text.Rotherham: I laid a snare for thee yea and thou wast captured O Babylon, when, thou wast not aware,—Thou wast found out yea and taken, For with Yahweh, hadst thou contended.

text.NRSV: The Lord has opened his armory, and brought out the weapons of his wrath, for the Lord God of hosts has a task to do in the land of the Chaldeans.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh opened his armoury, and brought out his weapons of indignation,—For, a work, it is pertaining to My Lord, Yahweh of hosts, in the land of the Chaldeans,

text.NRSV: Come against her from every quarter; open her granaries; pile her up like heaps of grain, and utterly destroy her; let nothing be left of her.
text.Rotherham: Come ye against her from farthest parts, Throw open her granaries, Cast her up as heaps, and devote her to destruction,—Do not let her have a remnant.

text.NRSV: Kill all her bulls, let them go down to the slaughter. Alas for them, for their day has come, the time of their punishment!
text.Rotherham: Cut up all her bullocks, Let them go down to the slaughter,—Alas for them! For their day, hath come, Their time for punishment.

text.NRSV: Listen! Fugitives and refugees from the land of Babylon are coming to Zion to declare the vengeance of the Lord our God, vengeance for his temple.
text.Rotherham: The voice Of them who are in flight and Of such as are escaping, out of the land of Babylon,—To tell in Zion The avenging of Yahweh our God, The avenging of his temple.

text.NRSV: Summon archers against Babylon, all who bend the bow. Encamp all around her; let no one escape. Repay her according to her deeds; just as she has done, do to her—for she has arrogantly defied the Lord, the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Publish against Babylon ye chiefs of all who tread the bow—Encamp against her round about Let there be none to escape, Recompense to her according to her work, According to all which she did, do ye to her,—For Against Yahweh, hath she acted presumptuously, Against the Holy One of Israel.

text.NRSV: Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares, and all her soldiers shall be destroyed on that day, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shall her young men fall in her broadways,—And, all her men of war, be silenced in that day, Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: I am against you, O arrogant one, says the Lord God of hosts; for your day has come, the time when I will punish you.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against thee most insolent one, Declareth My Lord Yahweh of hosts; For thy day, hath come, Thy time for punishment;

text.NRSV: The arrogant one shall stumble and fall, with no one to raise him up, and I will kindle a fire in his cities, and it will devour everything around him.
text.Rotherham: So shall the most insolent one stumble and fall, And he shall have none to lift him up,—And I will kindle a fire in his cities which shall devour all who are round about him.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord of hosts: The people of Israel are oppressed, and the people of Judah too; all who took them captive shut them up, they refuse to let them go.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts—Oppressed were the sons of Israel and the sons of Judah, together,—And, all who took them captive, Held them fast Refused to let them go:—

text.NRSV: Their Redeemer is strong; the Lord of hosts is his name. He will surely plead their cause, that he may give rest to the earth, but unrest to the inhabitants of Babylon.
text.Rotherham: Their Redeemer, can hold fast, Yahweh of hosts, is his name, He will, thoroughly plead, their plea,—That he may quiet the earth, And disquiet the inhabitants of Babylon.

text.NRSV: A sword against the Chaldeans, says the Lord, and against the inhabitants of Babylon, and against her officials and her sages!
text.Rotherham: A sword, is over the Chaldeans, Declareth Yahweh,—And against the inhabitants of Babylon, And against her princes, And against her wise men:

text.NRSV: A sword against the diviners, so that they may become fools! A sword against her warriors, so that they may be destroyed!
text.Rotherham: A sword, is against the praters, And they shall be shewn to be foolish, A sword, is against her heroes And they shall be dismayed:

text.NRSV: A sword against her horses and against her chariots, and against all the mixed peoples in her midst, so that they may become women! A sword against her treasures, so that they may be plundered!
text.Rotherham: A sword, is against his horses and against his chariots and, against all the rabble that are in her midst And they shall become women,—A sword, is against her treasures And they shall be made a prey:

text.NRSV: A drought against her waters, so that they may be dried up! For it is a land of images, and they go mad over idols.
text.Rotherham: A drought, is against her waters, And they shall be dried up,—For a land of images, it is, And with their shocking things, they act as men who are mad:

text.NRSV: Therefore wild animals shall live with hyenas in Babylon, and ostriches shall inhabit her; she shall never again be peopled, or inhabited for all generations.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall the criers dwell with, the howlers, Yea ostriches, shall dwell therein,—So shall it be dwelt in no more for ever, Neither shall it be inhabited unto generation after generation.

text.NRSV: As when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighbors, says the Lord, so no one shall live there, nor shall anyone settle in her.
text.Rotherham: Like the divine overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and her neighbours, Declareth Yahweh,—There shall not dwell there—a man, Nor sojourn therein—a son of the earth-born.

text.NRSV: Look, a people is coming from the north; a mighty nation and many kings are stirring from the farthest parts of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Lo! a people coming in from the North,—Yea, a great nation and many kings, shall be roused up out of the remote parts of the earth:

text.NRSV: They wield bow and spear, they are cruel and have no mercy. The sound of them is like the roaring sea; they ride on horses, arrayed as a warrior for battle against you, O daughter Babylon!
text.Rotherham: Bow and javelin, shall they grasp, Cruel, are they and will not have compassion, Their voice, like the sea, will roar, And, on horses, will they ride,—Set in array, as one man for battle, Against thee, O daughter of Babylon!

text.NRSV: The king of Babylon heard news of them, and his hands fell helpless; anguish seized him, pain like that of a woman in labor.
text.Rotherham: The king of Babylon hath heard the report of them, And unnerved are his hands,—Anguish, hath seized him, Writhing pain, as a woman in child-birth.

text.NRSV: Like a lion coming up from the thickets of the Jordan against a perennial pasture, I will suddenly chase them away from her; and I will appoint over her whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who can summon me? Who is the shepherd who can stand before me?
text.Rotherham: Lo! as a lion, shall he come up from the majesty of the Jordan, Unto the pasture perennial, But I will wink—I will make them run away therefrom, Who then shall be the Chosen One whom against it, I may set in charge? For who shall be like unto, me? And who shall appoint me, a time? And who is the Shepherd that shall stand before me?

text.NRSV: Therefore hear the plan that the Lord has made against Babylon and the purposes he has formed against the land of the Chaldeans: Surely the little ones of the flock shall be dragged away; surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.
text.Rotherham: Therefore hear ye, The counsel of Yahweh, which he hath counselled against Babylon, And his devices which he hath devised against the land of the Chaldeans,—Surely the little ones of the flock, shall drag them away, Surely he will cause the pasture to be astounded over them.

text.NRSV: At the sound of the capture of Babylon the earth shall quake, and a cry shall be heard among the nations.
text.Rotherham: At the noise of the taking of Babylon, the earth trembled,—And, the outcry, among the nations, was heard.

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord: See, I am going to stir up a destructive wind against Babylon and against the inhabitants of Leb-qamai;
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh—Behold me! stirring up against Babylon, And against the inhabitants of the centre of them who rise up against me,—A wind that destroyeth;

text.NRSV: and I will send winnowers to Babylon, and they shall winnow her. They shall empty her land; for they are against her from every side on the day of trouble.
text.Rotherham: And I will send to Babylon winnowers, And they shall winnow her, And shall empty her land,—For they are against her round about in the day of calamity.

text.NRSV: Let not the archer bend his bow, and let him not array himself in his coat of mail. Do not spare her young men; utterly destroy her entire army.
text.Rotherham: Let not the archer tread his bow, Nor lift himself up in his coat of mail,—And do not spare her young men, Devote to destruction all her host.

text.NRSV: They shall fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans, and pierced through in her streets.
text.Rotherham: So shall they fall wounded in the land of Chaldea,—Yea thrust through, in her streets,

text.NRSV: For Israel and Judah have not been forsaken by their God, the Lord of hosts, though their land is full of guilt before the Holy One of Israel.
text.Rotherham: For Israel and Judah have not been widowed Of their God, Of Yahweh of hosts,—But, their land, hath been filled with punishment for guilt from the Holy One of Israel

text.NRSV: Flee from the midst of Babylon, and save your lives, each of you! Do not perish because of her guilt, for this is the time of the Lord’s vengeance; he is repaying her what is due.
text.Rotherham: Flee out of the midst of Babylon And deliver ye every man his own life, Be not cut off in her punishment,—For it is Yahweh’s, time of avenging, A recompense, is he repaying unto her.

text.NRSV: Babylon was a golden cup in the Lord’s hand, making all the earth drunken; the nations drank of her wine, and so the nations went mad.
text.Rotherham: A cup of gold, was Babylon in the hand of Yahweh, Making drunk all the earth,—Of her wine, have the nations drunk, For this cause have the nations been acting as men who are mad.

text.NRSV: Suddenly Babylon has fallen and is shattered; wail for her! Bring balm for her wound; perhaps she may be healed.
text.Rotherham: Suddenly, hath Babylon fallen and been broken,—Howl ye over her Fetch balsam for her pain, Peradventure she shall be healed!

text.NRSV: “We tried to heal Babylon, but she could not be healed. Forsake her, and let us each go to our own country; for her judgment has reached up to heaven and has been lifted up even to the skies.”
text.Rotherham: We would have healed Babylon, but she is not healed, Leave her and let us go every one to his own land,—For her judgment, reacheth unto the heavens, And mounteth as far as the skies.

text.NRSV: The Lord has brought forth our vindication; come, let us declare in Zion the work of the Lord our God.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh hath brought forth our righteousnesses,—Come and let us relate in Zion the work of Yahweh our God.

text.NRSV: Sharpen the arrows! Fill the quivers! The Lord has stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes, because his purpose concerning Babylon is to destroy it, for that is the vengeance of the Lord, vengeance for his temple.
text.Rotherham: Polish the arrows, Lay hold of the shields,—Yahweh hath roused the spirit of the kings of the Medes, For against Babylon, his purpose is, to destroy her,—For it is, The avenging of Yahweh, The avenging of his temple.

text.NRSV: Raise a standard against the walls of Babylon; make the watch strong; post sentinels; prepare the ambushes; for the Lord has both planned and done what he spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon.
text.Rotherham: Against the walls of Babylon, Lift ye up a standard, Strengthen ye the watch, Station the watchmen, Make ready the ambuscades,—For Yahweh hath both planned and also performed that which he had spoken against the inhabitants of Babylon.

text.NRSV: You who live by many waters, rich in treasures, your end has come, the thread of your life is cut.
text.Rotherham: O thou who dwellest upon many waters, Who aboundest in treasures,—Come hath thine end! The measure of thine unrighteous gain!

text.NRSV: The Lord of hosts has sworn by himself: Surely I will fill you with troops like a swarm of locusts, and they shall raise a shout of victory over you.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh of hosts hath sworn, by his own soul: Surely I have filled thee with men as with locusts, And they have answered against thee with a shout.

text.NRSV: It is he who made the earth by his power, who established the world by his wisdom, and by his understanding stretched out the heavens.
text.Rotherham: He that Made the earth in his might, Established the world in his wisdom,—And in his understanding, stretched out the heavens

text.NRSV: When he utters his voice, there is a tumult of waters in the heavens, and he makes the mist rise from the ends of the earth. He makes lightnings for the rain, and he brings out the wind from his storehouses.
text.Rotherham: By the voice that he uttered, there was a tumult of waters in the heavens, And he caused vapours to ascend from the end of the earth,—Lightnings for the rain, made he, And brought forth wind out of his treasures.

text.NRSV: Everyone is stupid and without knowledge; goldsmiths are all put to shame by their idols; for their images are false, and there is no breath in them.
text.Rotherham: Every son of earth had become too brutish to know,—Every goldsmith, hath been put to shame, by a graven image,—For a falsehood, is his molten image Seeing there is no breath in them:

text.NRSV: They are worthless, a work of delusion; at the time of their punishment they shall perish.
text.Rotherham: Vanity, they are, A handiwork of mockeries,—In the time of their visitation, shall they perish.

text.NRSV: Not like these is the Lord, the portion of Jacob, for he is the one who formed all things, and Israel is the tribe of his inheritance; the Lord of hosts is his name.
text.Rotherham: Not like these, is the portion of Jacob, For the fashioner of all things, is he! And the portion of his inheritance,—Yahweh of hosts, is his name.

text.NRSV: “You are my war club, my weapon of battle: with you I smash nations; with you I destroy kingdoms;
text.Rotherham: A war-club, art thou for me, Weapons of war; Therefore will I Beat down with thee—nations, and Destroy with thee—kingdoms;

text.NRSV: with you I smash the horse and its rider; with you I smash the chariot and the charioteer;
text.Rotherham: and Beat down with thee—the horse and his rider,—and Beat down with thee—the chariot and its rider;

text.NRSV: with you I smash man and woman; with you I smash the old man and the boy; with you I smash the young man and the girl;
text.Rotherham: and Beat down with thee—man and woman, and Beat down with thee—elder and youth, and Beat down with thee—young man and virgin;

text.NRSV: with you I smash the shepherd and his flock; with you I smash the farmer and his team; with you I smash governors and deputies.
text.Rotherham: and Beat down with thee—the shepherd and his flock, and Beat down with thee—the plowman and his yoke;—and Beat down with thee—governors and deputies:

text.NRSV: “I will repay Babylon and all the inhabitants of Chaldea before your very eyes for all the wrong that they have done in Zion, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So will I recompense to Babylon, And to all the inhabitants of Chaldea, All their wickedness which they have committed against Zion before your eyes,—Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: “I am against you, O destroying mountain, says the Lord, that destroys the whole earth; I will stretch out my hand against you, and roll you down from the crags, and make you a burned-out mountain.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! against thee, O destroying mountain Declareth Yahweh, That destroyest all the earth,—Therefore will I stretch out my hand over thee, And roll thee down from the crags, And make of thee a burning mountain:

text.NRSV: No stone shall be taken from you for a cornerstone and no stone for a foundation, but you shall be a perpetual waste, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So shall they not fetch from thee A stone for a corner nor A stone for a foundation,—For desolations age-abiding, shalt thou become Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: “Raise a standard in the land, blow the trumpet among the nations; prepare the nations for war against her, summon against her the kingdoms, Ararat, Minni, and Ashkenaz; appoint a marshal against her, bring up horses like bristling locusts.
text.Rotherham: Set ye up an ensign in the earth Blow ye a horn among the nations Hallow against her—nations, Summon against her the kingdoms of Ararat Minni, and Ashkenaz,—Set in charge against her a marshal, Bring up cavalry like hairy locusts:

text.NRSV: Prepare the nations for war against her, the kings of the Medes, with their governors and deputies, and every land under their dominion.
text.Rotherham: Hallow against her—nations, With the kings of Media, With her governors and all her deputies,—And with all the land of his dominion:

text.NRSV: The land trembles and writhes in pain, for the Lord’s purposes against Babylon stand, to make the land of Babylon a desolation, without inhabitant.
text.Rotherham: Then did the land tremble and was in pain,—For the plans of Yahweh, had been established against Babylon, To make the land of Babylon an astonishment, without inhabitant.

text.NRSV: The warriors of Babylon have given up fighting, they remain in their strongholds; their strength has failed, they have become women; her buildings are set on fire, her bars are broken.
text.Rotherham: The heroes of Babylon have ceased to fight They have remained in the strongholds, Parched is their might, They have become women,—They have set fire to her habitations, Broken are her bars!

text.NRSV: One courier runs to meet another, and one messenger to meet another, to tell the king of Babylon that his city is taken from end to end:
text.Rotherham: Runner to meet runner, shall they run, And teller to meet teller,-To tell the king of Babylon, That captured is his city at the end!

text.NRSV: the fords have been seized, the marshes have been burned with fire, and the soldiers are in a panic.
text.Rotherham: and The fords, have been seized, and The reeds, have they burned with fire; and The men of war, are dismayed!

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel: Daughter Babylon is like a threshing floor at the time when it is trodden; in a little while the time of her harvest will come.
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, God of Israel, The daughter of Babylon, is like a threshing, floor at the time of treading her: Yet a little, and the time of harvest shall overtake her.

text.NRSV: “King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon has devoured me and crushed me; he has made me an empty vessel, he has swallowed me like a monster; he has filled his belly with my delicacies, he has spewed me out.
text.Rotherham: Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon—Hath devoured me Hath vexed me, Hath set me down as an empty vessel, He hath swallowed me like a sea-monster, He hath filled his belly with my dainties,—He hath driven me away!

text.NRSV: May my torn flesh be upon Babylon,” the inhabitant of Zion shall say. “May my blood be upon the inhabitants of Chaldea,” Jerusalem shall say.
text.Rotherham: The violence done to me and to my flesh, be upon Babylon! Shall the inhabitress of Zion say,—Yea, my blood, be upon the inhabitant of Chaldea! Shall Jerusalem say.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord: I am going to plead your cause and take vengeance for you. I will dry up her sea and make her fountain dry;
text.Rotherham: Therefore, Thus, saith Yahweh, Behold me! pleading thy cause, So then I will execute the avenging of thee; And will dry up her sea, And make dry her spring:

text.NRSV: and Babylon shall become a heap of ruins, a den of jackals, an object of horror and of hissing, without inhabitant.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall Babylon become—Heaps A habitation of jackals An astonishment, and A hissing, Without inhabitant.

text.NRSV: They shall roar together like lions; they shall growl like lions’ whelps.
text.Rotherham: Together, like wild lions, shall they roar,—They have growled like lions’ whelps.

text.NRSV: When they are inflamed, I will set them a feast and make them drunk, until they become merry and then sleep a perpetual sleep and never awake, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: When they are heated, I will spread their banquets, And let them drink that they may become uproarious, So shall they sleep an age-abiding sleep and not wake,—Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams and goats.
text.Rotherham: I will bring them down As fat lambs to slaughter,—As rams with he-goats.

text.NRSV: “How Sheshach is taken, the pride of the whole earth seized! How Babylon has become an object of horror among the nations!
text.Rotherham: How hath Sheshach, been captured! How hath the praise of all the earth been seized! How hath Babylon become an astonishment among the nations!

text.NRSV: The sea has risen over Babylon; she has been covered by its roaring waves.
text.Rotherham: The sea, hath gone up over Babylon,—With the multitude of its rolling waves, is she covered.

text.NRSV: Her towns have become a desolation, a dry land and a desert, a land in which no one lives, and through which no mortal passes.
text.Rotherham: Her cities have become, an astonishment, A land parched up, and a waste plain,—A land wherein shall no man dwell, Nor pass through them a son of the earth-born!

text.NRSV: I will punish Bel in Babylon, and make him disgorge what he has swallowed. The nations shall no longer stream to him; the wall of Babylon has fallen.
text.Rotherham: So will I bring punishment upon Bel in Babylon, And will bring forth what he hath swallowed out of his mouth, And the nations shall stream unto him no more,—Even the wall of Babylon hath fallen!

text.NRSV: “Come out of her, my people! Save your lives, each of you, from the fierce anger of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Come ye forth out of her midst O my people, And deliver ye every man his own life,—Because of the glow of the anger of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Do not be fainthearted or fearful at the rumors heard in the land—one rumor comes one year and another the next, a rumor of violence in the land and of ruler against ruler.
text.Rotherham: And let not your heart be timid, nor be ye afraid, Because of the report that is reported in the earth When there shall come in one year, the report And after that in another year, the report, With violence in the earth, and ruler upon ruler.

text.NRSV: “Therefore the days are surely coming when I will punish the images of Babylon; her whole land shall be put to shame, and all her slain shall fall in her midst.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days coming when I will bring punishment upon the images of Babylon, And all her land, shall turn pale,—Yea, all her wounded, shall fall in her midst.

text.NRSV: Then the heavens and the earth, and all that is in them, shall shout for joy over Babylon; for the destroyers shall come against her out of the north, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall shout over Babylon—heavens and earth and all who are therein,—For out of the North, shall come to her the spoilers Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Babylon must fall for the slain of Israel, as the slain of all the earth have fallen because of Babylon.
text.Rotherham: Not only hath Babylon, caused the fall of the slain of Israel,—By Babylon, also have fallen the slain of all the earth.

text.NRSV: “You who have escaped from the sword, go, do not linger! Remember the Lord from far away, and let Jerusalem come into your mind:
text.Rotherham: Ye that have escaped the sword, depart do not stand still,—Remember, from afar Yahweh, Let, Jerusalem, come up on your heart:-

text.NRSV: “We are put to shame, for we have heard insults; dishonor has covered our faces, for strangers have come into the holy places of the Lord’s house.”
text.Rotherham: We have turned pale for we have heard a reproach, Confusion, hath covered our faces,—For aliens have entered upon the hallowed places of the house of Yahweh!

text.NRSV: “Therefore the days are surely coming, says the Lord, when I will punish her images, and through all her land the wounded shall groan.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! days are coming, Declareth Yahweh, When I will bring punishment upon her images; And throughout all her land, shall the pierced one groan.

text.NRSV: Though Babylon should mount up to heaven, and though she should fortify her strong height, from me destroyers would come upon her, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Though Babylon should mount the heavens, And though she should fortify her strong high-place, From me, should come spoilers unto her Declareth Yahweh.

text.NRSV: “Listen! —a cry from Babylon! A great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans!
text.Rotherham: A voice of outcry from Babylon! And a great crash from the land of the Chaldeans!

text.NRSV: For the Lord is laying Babylon waste, and stilling her loud voice. Their waves roar like mighty waters, the noise of their voice is raised;
text.Rotherham: For, Yahweh, is spoiling Babylon, And will destroy out of her the loud voice,—Though their waves have roared like many waters, Been uttered the loud boast of their voice.

text.NRSV: for a destroyer has come against her, against Babylon; her warriors are taken, their bows are broken; for the Lord is a God of recompense, he will surely repay.
text.Rotherham: For there hath come upon her—upon Babylon—a spoiler, And captured are their heroes And broken are their bows,—For, a GOD of recompenses, is Yahweh, He will surely repay.

text.NRSV: I will make her officials and her sages drunk, also her governors, her deputies, and her warriors; they shall sleep a perpetual sleep and never awake, says the King, whose name is the Lord of hosts.
text.Rotherham: Then will I make drunk—Her princes and her wise men, Her governors, and her deputies and her heroes, And they shall sleep an age-abiding sleep, and not wake,—Declareth the King, Yahweh of hosts, is his name.

text.NRSV: “Thus says the Lord of hosts: The broad wall of Babylon shall be leveled to the ground, and her high gates shall be burned with fire. The peoples exhaust themselves for nothing, and the nations weary themselves only for fire.”
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts—The broad walls of Babylon, shall be laid utterly bare, And, her lofty gates, with fire, shall be burned,—And peoples shall labour for emptiness, And populations, for the fire, shall weary themselves.

text.NRSV: The word that the prophet Jeremiah commanded Seraiah son of Neriah son of Mahseiah, when he went with King Zedekiah of Judah to Babylon, in the fourth year of his reign. Seraiah was the quartermaster.
text.Rotherham: The word which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah son of Neriah son of Mahseiah, when he went with Zedekiah king of Judah into Babylon, in the fourth year of his reign,—now Seraiah, was travelling-marshal.

text.NRSV: Jeremiah wrote in a scroll all the disasters that would come on Babylon, all these words that are written concerning Babylon.
text.Rotherham: So Jeremiah wrote all the calamity which was to come unto Babylon in one scroll; even all these words which have been written against Babylon.

text.NRSV: And Jeremiah said to Seraiah: “When you come to Babylon, see that you read all these words,
text.Rotherham: Then said Jeremiah unto Seraiah,—When thou comest into Babylon, then shalt thou look out and read all these words;

text.NRSV: and say, ‘O Lord, you yourself threatened to destroy this place so that neither human beings nor animals shall live in it, and it shall be desolate forever.’
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say—O Yahweh! thou, thyself, hast spoken against this place to cut it off, That there be in it no inhabitant Neither man nor beast,—But desolations age-abiding, shall it become!

text.NRSV: When you finish reading this scroll, tie a stone to it, and throw it into the middle of the Euphrates,
text.Rotherham: And it shall be when thou hast made an end of reading this scroll, that thou shalt bind thereunto a stone, and cast it in the midst of the Euphrates,

text.NRSV: and say, ‘Thus shall Babylon sink, to rise no more, because of the disasters that I am bringing on her.’ ” Thus far are the words of Jeremiah.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou say,—In like manner, shall Babylon sink and not rise Because of the calamity which I am about to bring thereupon: So shall they perish. Thus far, are, the words of Jeremiah.

text.NRSV: Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he began to reign; he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
text.Rotherham: Twenty-one years old, was Zedekiah when he began to reign, and eleven years, reigned he, in Jerusalem,—and, his mother’s name, was Hamutal, daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, just as Jehoiakim had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—according to all that Jehoiakim had done.

text.NRSV: Indeed, Jerusalem and Judah so angered the Lord that he expelled them from his presence. Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
text.Rotherham: For it was, because the anger of Yahweh, had come against Jerusalem and Judah until he had cast them out from his presence, that Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.

text.NRSV: And in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, King Nebuchadrezzar of Babylon came with all his army against Jerusalem, and they laid siege to it; they built siegeworks against it all around.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth of the month, that Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon came, he and all his force, against Jerusalem, and encamped against it,—and he built against it a siege-wall round about.

text.NRSV: So the city was besieged until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
text.Rotherham: And the city came into the siege,—until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.

text.NRSV: On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine became so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
text.Rotherham: In the fourth month on the ninth of the month, when the famine had become severe in the city,—and there had come to be no bread for the people of the land,

text.NRSV: Then a breach was made in the city wall; and all the soldiers fled and went out from the city by night by the way of the gate between the two walls, by the king’s garden, though the Chaldeans were all around the city. They went in the direction of the Arabah.
text.Rotherham: then was the city broken up, and all the men of war, beginning to flee went forth out of the city by night, by way of the gate between the two walls which was by the garden of the king ()the Chaldeans being near the city round about),—and they went the way towards the Waste Plain.

text.NRSV: But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king, and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and all his army was scattered, deserting him.
text.Rotherham: And the force of the Chaldeans pursued the king, and overtook Zedekiah, in the Waste Plains of Jericho,—and, all his force, was scattered from him.

text.NRSV: Then they captured the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath, and he passed sentence on him.
text.Rotherham: So they seized the king, and brought him up, unto the king of Babylon at Riblah, in the land of Hamath,—and he pronounced upon him sentences of judgment.

text.NRSV: The king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes, and also slaughtered all the officers of Judah at Riblah.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes,—moreover also all the princes of Judah, slew he in Riblah;

text.NRSV: He put out the eyes of Zedekiah, and bound him in fetters of bronze; the king of Babylon took him to Babylon and put him in prison until the day of his death.
text.Rotherham: and the eyes of Zedekiah, put he out,—and bound him with fetters of bronze and the king of Babylon took him to Babylon, and put him in prison—until the day of his death.

text.NRSV: In the fifth month, on the tenth day of the month—which was the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadrezzar, king of Babylon—Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard who served the king of Babylon, entered Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: And in the fifth month on the tenth of the month, the same, was the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadrezzar, king of Babylon, came Nebuzaradan, chief of the royal executioners,—who stood before the king of Babylon into, Jerusalem;

text.NRSV: He burned the house of the Lord, the king’s house, and all the houses of Jerusalem; every great house he burned down.
text.Rotherham: and he burned the house of Yahweh and the house of the king,—yea all the houses of Jerusalem even every great man’s house, burned he with fire;

text.NRSV: All the army of the Chaldeans, who were with the captain of the guard, broke down all the walls around Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: and all the walls of Jerusalem round about, did all the force of the Chaldeans who were with the chief of the royal executioners, break down.

text.NRSV: Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried into exile some of the poorest of the people and the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had defected to the king of Babylon, together with the rest of the artisans.
text.Rotherham: And some of the poor of the people and the residue of the people who were left in the city, and the disheartened who had fallen away unto the king of Babylon and the residue of the multitude, did Nebuzaradan chief of the royal executioners carry away captive.

text.NRSV: But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest people of the land to be vinedressers and tillers of the soil.
text.Rotherham: But, others of the poor of the land, did Nebuzaradan chief of the royal executioners leave, for vinedressers and for husbandmen.

text.NRSV: The bronze pillars that were in the house of the Lord, and the stands and the bronze sea that were in the house of the Lord, the Chaldeans broke in pieces, and carried all the bronze to Babylon.
text.Rotherham: And the pillars of bronze that pertained to the house of Yahweh and the stands and the sea of bronze which was in the house of Yahweh, did the Chaldeans, break in pieces,—and they carried away all the bronze of them to Babylon;

text.NRSV: They took the pots, the shovels, the snuffers, the basins, the ladles, and all the bronze vessels used in the temple service.
text.Rotherham: and the caldrons and the shovels and the snuffers and the dashing bowls, and the spoons even all the utensils of bronze wherewith ministration used to be made did they take away;

text.NRSV: The captain of the guard took the bowls, the firepans, the basins, the pots, the lampstands, the ladles, and the cups, all that were made of gold and all that were made of silver.
text.Rotherham: and the basins and the censers and the dashing bowls and the caldrons and the lamps and the spoons and the cups, which were of gold in gold, and which were of silver in silver, did the chief of the royal executioners, take away.

text.NRSV: As for the two pillars, the one sea, the twelve bronze bulls that were under the sea, and the stands, which King Solomon had made for the house of the Lord, the bronze of all these vessels was beyond weighing.
text.Rotherham: As for the two pillars the one sea, and the twelve oxen of bronze which were under the stands which King Solomon had made for the house of Yahweh, without weight was the bronze of all these things.

text.NRSV: As for the pillars, the height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits, its circumference was twelve cubits; it was hollow and its thickness was four fingers.
text.Rotherham: Now as for the pillars, eighteen cubits, was the height of each pillar, and, a line of twelve cubits, compassed it about,—and the thickness thereof was four fingers’ breadth—hollow;

text.NRSV: Upon it was a capital of bronze; the height of the one capital was five cubits; a network and pomegranates, all of bronze, were on the capital all around. And the second pillar had the same, with pomegranates.
text.Rotherham: and there was, a capital, upon it, of bronze, and the height of each capital, was five cubits, with lattice-work and pomegranates upon the capital round about—the whole was of bronze,—and like these, were the second pillar and the pomegranates.

text.NRSV: There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides; all the pomegranates surrounding the network were one hundred.
text.Rotherham: And the pomegranates were ninety-six on a side,—all the pomegranates were a hundred upon the lattice-work round about.

text.NRSV: The captain of the guard took the chief priest Seraiah, the second priest Zephaniah, and the three guardians of the threshold;
text.Rotherham: And the chief of the royal executioners, took away, Seraiah the first priest, and Zephaniah, the second priest,—and the three keepers of the entrance-hall;

text.NRSV: and from the city he took an officer who had been in command of the soldiers, and seven men of the king’s council who were found in the city; the secretary of the commander of the army who mustered the people of the land; and sixty men of the people of the land who were found inside the city.
text.Rotherham: and out of the city, took he one eunuch who was in charge over the men of war and seven men of them who used to watch the face of the king, who were found in the city, and the scribe of the prince of the host, who used to muster the people of the land,—and sixty men of the people of the land, who were found in the midst of the city.

text.NRSV: Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
text.Rotherham: And when Nebuzaradan chief the royal executioners, had taken them, and brought them unto the king of Babylon at Riblah,

text.NRSV: And the king of Babylon struck them down, and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. So Judah went into exile out of its land.
text.Rotherham: then did the king of Babylon smite them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath,—thus carried he Judah captive away from off their own soil.

text.NRSV: This is the number of the people whom Nebuchadrezzar exiled: in the seventh year, three thousand twenty-three Judeans;
text.Rotherham: This, is the people, whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive,—In the seventh year—of them of Judah, three thousand and twenty-three;

text.NRSV: in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar he exiled from Jerusalem eight hundred thirty-two persons;
text.Rotherham: In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar—out of Jerusalem, eight hundred and thirty-two souls;

text.NRSV: in the twenty-third year of Nebuchadrezzar, Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard exiled of the Judeans seven hundred forty-five persons; all the persons were four thousand six hundred.
text.Rotherham: In the three-and-twentieth year of Nebuchadrezzar, Nebuzaradan, chief of the royal executioners took away captive, of them of Judah, seven hundred and forty-five souls: All, the souls, were four thousand and six hundred.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-seventh year of the exile of King Jehoiachin of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth day of the month, King Evil-merodach of Babylon, in the year he began to reign, showed favor to King Jehoiachin of Judah and brought him out of prison;
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth of the month, that Evil-merodach king of Babylon, in the year he began to reign, lifted up, the head of Jehoiachin king of Judah, and brought him forth out of prison;

text.NRSV: he spoke kindly to him, and gave him a seat above the seats of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
text.Rotherham: and spake with him comfortable words,—and set his throne above the throne of the kings who were with him in Babylon;

text.NRSV: So Jehoiachin put aside his prison clothes, and every day of his life he dined regularly at the king’s table.
text.Rotherham: so he changed his prison garments,—and used to eat bread before his face continually, all the days of his life.

text.NRSV: For his allowance, a regular allowance was given him by the king of Babylon, a portion every day until the day of his death, as long as he lived.
text.Rotherham: And his allowance, was a continual allowance given him from the king of Babylon the portion of the day upon its own day, until the day of his death,—all the days of his life.
